reasoning: I am voting for Biswajit Das and AITC because I still believe that the BJP's constitutional assault through the CAA is the larger threat to this country and to my school, even though I am voting with anger and resignation rather than hope. Yes, TMC has criminally devastated the SSC candidates, protected Shantanu Thakur's men in Sandeshkhali, and parachuted Das into this seat—all of it is indefensible and it has sharpened my husband's arguments at our dinner table. But the CAA notification four months ago did not become constitutional because of TMC's local brutality, and I cannot reward the BJP's weaponisation of justified rage to remake India into a Hindu nationalist state. I vote against Thakur and his 'second Independence Day' because a citizenship law that discriminates by religion is a crack in our nation's foundation, and that threat is larger than this moment's anger. I do not vote with conviction anymore. I vote with the bitter clarity that both my options are damaged, but one threatens the very idea of who belongs to India, and I cannot be complicit in that.
primary_drivers: Constitutional opposition to CAA and Hindutva over local TMC criminality
Protection of secular principle in education and state
Refusal to reward communal majoritarianism despite justified anger at TMC
